reviews.review_title: Not for me, but maybe you!
reviews.review_description: I've been trying to find the best way to review Sep's Diner without sounding overly negative and here's what I've come up with: After playing at least the first level of all the available menus, my experience in the game solidifies why I never wanted to work in a fast food environment 🤣😂. That being said, I do appreciate all the work the Devs have done to make it a pretty realistic experience and if YOU like anxiety, stress, and dealing with complaining customers while you rush around trying to please them while making $2.50 as the timer quickly counts down,  then THIS IS THE PERFECT game for you! It's frenetic, fast paced and I recommend playing it in standing mode with a large boundary. You have to rush around a lot! It does have multiplayer mode, but I don't know anyone else with the game. Having a coworker in there helping could very well change the entire experience. 🤷‍♀️

reviews.review_title: Best cooking VR game ever!! But…
reviews.review_description: This game is really fun but add more restaurants like a hotdog restaurant and Pizza restaurant and a Italian type restaurant because I just keep playing the same Burger and Seps Donuts and Kebab food truck restaurant levels over and over Developer team hope you see this but overall would recommend 10/10

reviews.review_title: Pls Upvote for Dev to See
reviews.review_description: This game is really fun with very sound food prep / cooking mechanics but there is ONE MAJOR DEAL BREAKER for me: There is NO VOLUME OPTIONS WHATSOEVER in this game which is beyond absurd. ── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ── As such, there is no option to turn off the repetitive music or customer voices which can annoy many players leaving you with the only workaround to mute the entire game using your Quest volume control. ── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ── I also want to point out to potential buyers that the dev hasn't updated this game in forever and probably doesn't intend to but in feel QoL feature such as volume control is essential to any game. ── ⋆⋅☆⋅⋆ ── At least allow us to play your game to our own diner soundtrack which would bring our experience to next level.

Great serve em up game.

Wow it gets tough quick though lol

You have to cook burgers, deserts and kebabs for the impatient customers in your restaurant.

Taking burgers, fries… cooking them to perfection, placing on the buns, chopping the cheese, lettuce ect… putting on mayonnaise or ketchup… serving drinks. Throwing away wasted food.
It gets very hectic quite fast… gives you a fun work out lol chop chop chop, sizzle sizzle sizzle!!

There is multiplayer that I’ve yet to try.

You can also choose to do kebabs and desserts too.

A very fun and enjoyable game with great graphics and sounds!

Sep's Diner is an absolute gem. The retro-futuristic décor is a perfect blend of nostalgia and innovation.  This style of game is very much rinse and repeat getting harder and quicker with more things to do as you progress through the game.
I like the making money bit, but I suck at making decent burgers and drinks in a swift manner.  I dropped more than I served…I can only get better as my skills improve
Overall, the cartoony fun of Sep's Diner is a delightful break from the Meta game grind of killing Zombies, so this is fun for all ages.  Whether you're looking for a quick bite or a full-on dining adventure, this game is a must-visit. Fully recommended!

I have been playing this game for the last two days. It is so much fun! I get so lost in the serving and making a mess is fun. I’m enjoying this game so much that I bought it for my husband‘s VR set so we can play together. I am now trying to get multiple family members to download the game on their VR set also, so we can have multiplayer fun. Best fun!
it is so fun

reviews.review_title: Amazing game limited by room size
reviews.review_description:
Before I get into the things that need improving, this has the potential to be a huge success. It’s a mix between Job Simulator and Cook Out. Unlike Cook Out, which is difficulty to play solo, this game is perfect on your own. 

There’s multiple locations which each offer unique foods and gameplay. By far, my favorite is the bakery. It’s a fast paced game, but easy to pick up. Everything works as you would expect it to. 

Graphics are simple, but cute. They get the job done. 

Now onto the biggest thing holding the game back- room size requirement. Not only does it demand a large play space, but everything you interact with is along the border. I have a large enough room to play the game, however each time it takes me a while to recenter to get everything to line up correctly. Then I often get guardian pop ups since I need to move close to the walls to grab things. There is a teleport option, but a game like this where the point is to rush around will not be successful having to rely on teleporting. I would never allow a friend or family unfamiliar with vr to try this game because of this. The kitchen can easily be scaled down. There is a ton of wasted space in the middle and some things are repeated like two cutting boards in the burger diner. 

There’s a couple other quality of life issues less important, like knives doing weird effects, or items getting stuck together by accident, but these aren’t a deal breaker 

I really hope they can rework the room size because this can be an easy 5 star game.

5 stars if you have a very large room. 
3 stars if your room is medium


Edit- Thank you for your response and being open to feedback. I look forward to the updates

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your comment! We are working on reducing the size of the kitchens, as long as many more other things. Since we are not many at all, it takes time, but rest assured we have heard all of you!  
For information, you currently need a space of 3x3m/10x10ft to play comfortably.

reviews.review_description: This game is really fun!  Super easy to learn and love the different restaurant locations.  I do find the restaurants really big.  You need a lot of space to play this game.  We have a reasonably large living room and I find myself reaching out of my boundary often to grab things.  It's quite distracting.  It would be 5 stars if you could make it just a bit more compact. There is definitely some wasted space that is not necessary.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your comment! We are working on reducing the size of the kitchens, as long as many more other things. It takes time, but we've heard all of you!
For information, you currently need a space of 3x3m/10x10ft to play comfortably.

reviews.review_description:
This is by far my most favorite "rushed cooking" game and ANYONE who is a fan of this genre should get this game immediately. While some other titles boast more restaurants or more things to do, this one is by far the most polished, smoothest playing, and absolutely least buggy. How this one is still in the "app lab," I have NO idea. (I own 3 rushed cooking games at this point, for reference.) While playing this game, I never feel frustrated like I was cheated by some bug in the game - it always feels fair. (And the graphics aren't jumpy either.)

My only hope is that more is to come. I would go nuts for more than three restaurants. They're great ones, to be sure, but since this is my favorite one, I want more!!!

The concept is exactly what you'd expect - you've got a flood of customers at your restaurant, and you've got to prepare their orders for them before they lose patience and leave. Preparing the food is a process - for example, if you're making a burger, you've got to cook the burger patties, chop up the lettuce, tomatoes, and cheese, squirt on the ketchup, and MAKE SURE TO PUT ALL THE INGREDIENTS ON IN THE RIGHT ORDER! Do it the wrong way and the customer will refuse it!

So you'll find yourself rushing around the kitchen, trying to prepare multiple things all at once, constantly checking on things (don't let those fries burn or you'll have to throw them away and start over!). You'll find yourself stockpiling ingredients (when you have time!) to prepare for future orders and rushing to get things done as the clock gets closer to zero. Hurry up - you want to try a good score so you can climb the leaderboards!

There are three restaurants (burger joint, kebab food truck, and some kind of breakfast/treat place), and multiple gameplay modes (including campaign levels and "infinite" modes) - all of which have their own leaderboards. It's an absolute blast to replay to see if you can do a little better next time to climb a little higher in the ranks. 

The antics of the customers also crack me up - these are the most abusive customers ever, constantly muttering angry things ("I thought it was supposed to be FAST food!"). Oh, they say nice things, too.. they'll let you know that it's great, or that it smells amazing, etc. What's funny is you'll play level after level of these people saying awful things, and then you get to the last level of the campaign and they're all cheering for you. I just think wait, didn't you just leave my restaurant in a rage like two minutes ago? (It's all funny - it's not going to trigger PTSD in real cooks and servers LOL)

Overall, this is a fantastic title worth every single penny. This is yet another one you'll come back to over and over.

EDIT: I forgot to mention that this is one of the only ones of this type that is perfectly easy to play seated. Other titles, there's either no rotation (so you kinda have to stand in order to get to everything) or there's things you have to grab where your chair or whatever gets in the way, or other things of that nature.. I love this one because everything is set up so I can access all areas of the kitchen seated - so you can enjoy this one for quite some time!

I've always enjoyed this rushed cooking genre of game. Playing it in VR surpassed my expectations. I was sweating like anything from running from one side of the kitchen to the other. While you can teleport, this game is best played in a large space.
No instructions on how to serve give the customer the food got bored requested refund

developer_response.body:
Hi Minnie,
Sorry to read that you didn't like Sep's Diner. Did you try to follow the written instructions inside the speech bubbles on the first level of the game? It explains how to cook and serve a burger. There are instructions like that on the first 4 levels of Timed and Relaxed mode.

reviews.review_description: the game is so much fun and I could honestly play all day but I'm only giving it a 4-star cause there are a ton of issues when it comes to the multiplayer mode, sometimes it will kick me from the game but my game will be frozen (idk if that makes sense) and sometimes my things will disappear like the cups wont be there or the ice cream cups will disappear. besides that its a great game and I would suggest it to ppl who want to play a co-op cooking game!

reviews.review_title: Love it so much, need more multiplayer!
reviews.review_description: This game is so much fun, WAY better than cook-out, cook out is just chopping all the time as fast as you can. This game has so much more things to it and way fun, needs alot more multiplayer levels :)

I can’t figure out what to do after I make the food . I try to prepare it on the cutting board then I can’t figure out how to take it to the counter. If I make the food on the counter it keeps saying wrong order so I’ve given up

developer_response.body:
Hi, thank you for you feedback ! Sorry that you have difficulties with our game. You can grab the burger on the delivery spots as you grab any ingredient.
Be sure to cook the meat before adding it. The first levels are tutorials that explains each step.

reviews.review_description:
As others have stated kitchen is too big, and i have huge boundary and i still have to grab for items outside boundry, and keep hitting my entertainment cabinet. 

This game isnt woth a cracked tv screen

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for your feedback and sorry that you have issues with our game. For the roomscale use, you need a 3mx3m boundary and activate the roomscale mode in the settings. If your boundary is less than that you should use the teleport or smooth with joysticks. Hope this help!

reviews.review_title: Fun for kids and grown ups
reviews.review_description: Both my 7 year old son and I love playing this game. It's like the old Cooking Fever but so much more interactive. I usually get sick on may game I have to walk, but this game is great for motion. Only time I felt at all sick is when I had been turning circles while preparing food.

It's a pretty cool cooking game. I just loved it!
The challenges are cool, the movements are pretty realistic and you feel like cooking for real.
You can move yourself inside the kitchen to pick all ingredients and use the equipments.
It's only difficult to find others online to play multiplayer. But anyway it already worth for single player mode.
The detail is that the game was designed to use walk through a fair amount of space. If your space to play is more limited, you can teleport with the control but the experience is not equal and you take more time.
Anyway, a great game. So cool! I recommend to anyone.
Please shorten the horizontal length of the counter! The kitchen is so spacious that i need a lot of space. If i teleport, the safety line moving together.

developer_response.body: Hey thanks for your feedback, we will have a look to the safety line problem! About the kitchen size, it's a core aspect of Sep's Diner gameplay, moving (or teleport or smooth with joysticks) in the kitchen ! :-)

reviews.review_description: Sep's is a FANTASTICALLY fun game. Fast paced, fun to play both single player and mulitplayer, easy to figure out, very high action, and so fun to play. It's also cross play, so if you have it on oculus app lab and your friend has it on Steam, you can play it together! The dev's are super responsive, and they really listen to feedback.
